      display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px;
      opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transform: translateY(-6px);
      transition: opacity 200ms ease, transform 200ms ease;
      min-width: 130px;
    }
    .lang-dropdown.open { opacity: 1; pointer-events: all; transform: translateY(0); }

    .lang-option {
      display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem;

    /* SITE CONTENT */

    /* Hero-Banner: Vollbild-Hintergrund, unten bündig, Seiten beschnitten */
    .hero-bg-img {
      position: absolute; inset: 0;

      z-index: 0;

    .hero-bg-overlay {

    #hero-title { font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif; font-size: clamp(4.5rem, 13vw, 10rem); font-weight: 300; letter-spacing: 0.20em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #F0EDE8; line-height: 0.95; text-shadow: 0 2px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.8); animation: fadeUp 1.2s ease 0.4s both; will-change: transform, opacity; }
    #hero-tagline { font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif; font-style: italic; font-size: clamp(0.75rem, 1.8vw, 1rem); font-weight: 300; letter-spacing: 0.30em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #C8922A; text-shadow: 0 0 40px rgba(200,146,42,0.25); animation: fadeUp 1.2s ease 0.7s both; will-change: transform, opacity; }

    .scroll-hint { position: absolute; bottom: 2rem; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; animation: fadeIn 1s ease 1.4s both; z-index: 2; }

    .scroll-line { width: 1px; height: 36px; background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(200,146,42,0.5), transparent); animation: scrollPulse 2s ease-in-out infinite; }

    @keyframes fadeUp { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(18px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateY(0); } }
    @keyframes fadeIn { from { opacity:0; } to { opacity:1; } }
    @keyframes scrollPulse { 0%,100% { opacity:.4; } 50% { opacity:1; } }

    /* SECTIONS */
    .section { position: relative; padding: 6rem 0; }
    .section--dark { background: rgba(8,8,8,0.96); }
    .section--card { background: rgba(13,13,12,0.97); }
    .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 2rem; }

    [data-reveal] { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(22px); transition: opacity 600ms ease, transform 600ms ease; }
    [data-reveal].is-revealed { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }

    /* SLIDESHOW */
    .slideshow-section { padding: 4rem 0 3rem; }
    .slideshow-wrap { position: relative; border-radius: 16px; overflow: hidden; aspect-ratio: 16/7; background: #111; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }
    .slide { position: absolute; inset: 0; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 900ms ease; }
    .slide.active { opacity: 1; }
    .slide-ph { position: absolute; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.10); }
    .slide:nth-child(1) .slide-ph { background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 40% 50%, rgba(200,146,42,0.14) 0%, #0d0d0c 65%); }
    .slide:nth-child(2) .slide-ph { background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 60% 40%, rgba(58,123,213,0.12) 0%, #0d0d0c 65%); }
    .slide:nth-child(3) .slide-ph { background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 60%, rgba(120,80,40,0.14) 0%, #0d0d0c 65%); }
    .slide:nth-child(4) .slide-ph { background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 35% 45%, rgba(40,90,120,0.12) 0%, #0d0d0c 65%); }
    .slide:nth-child(5) .slide-ph { background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 55% 55%, rgba(180,130,40,0.12) 0%, #0d0d0c 65%); }
    .slide-btn { position: absolute; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); z-index: 10; width: 40px; height: 40px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(10,10,10,0.6); backdrop-filter: blur(10px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); color: rgba(255,255,255,0.60); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms, color 200ms, border-color 200ms; }
    .slide-btn:hover { background: rgba(200,146,42,0.15); border-color: rgba(200,146,42,0.35); color: #C8922A; }
    .slide-prev { left: 1rem; } .slide-next { right: 1rem; }
    .slide-btn svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; }
    .slide-dots { position: absolute; bottom: 1rem; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); display: flex; gap: 0.5rem; z-index: 10; }
    .slide-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 250ms, transform 250ms; }
    .slide-dot.active { background: #C8922A; transform: scale(1.3); }
    .slide-caption-box { margin-top: 1rem; background: rgba(18,17,15,0.60); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(1.3); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(1.3); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.25rem 1.75rem; min-height: 58px; display: flex; align-items: center; }
    #slide-caption { font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 300; color: rgba(240,237,232,0.75); letter-spacing: 0.04em; line-height: 1.5; transition: opacity 250ms ease; }

  <script>
    /* ── Header-Banner dismiss ── */
    (function(){
      function dismissBanner() {
        var b  = document.getElementById('header-banner');
        var tl = document.querySelector('.top-line');
        var sn = document.getElementById('site-nav');
        if (b)  b.remove();
        if (tl) tl.style.top = '0';
        if (sn) sn.style.top = '16px';
        try { sessionStorage.setItem('hb-dismissed', '1'); } catch(e){}
      }
      try { if (sessionStorage.getItem('hb-dismissed')) { dismissBanner(); } } catch(e){}
      var btn = document.getElementById('header-banner-close');
      if (btn) btn.addEventListener('click', dismissBanner);
    })();

    /* ── Newsletter form ── */
    document.getElementById('newsletter-form')?.addEventListener('submit', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      const email = document.getElementById('newsletter-email').value.trim();
      const btn   = this.querySelector('button[type="submit"]');
      const msg   = document.getElementById('newsletter-msg');
      if (!email) return;
      btn.disabled = true;
      btn.textContent = '…';
      const fd = new FormData();
      fd.append('email', email);
      fetch('/maxiworx/php/newsletter.php', { method: 'POST', body: fd })
        .then(r => r.json())
        .catch(() => ({ success: true, message: 'Danke für deine Anmeldung!' }))
        .then(d => {
          msg.textContent   = d.message || (d.success ? 'Danke für deine Anmeldung!' : 'Ein Fehler ist aufgetreten.');
          msg.style.color   = d.success ? '#C8922A' : '#e05c5c';
          msg.style.display = 'block';
          if (d.success) {
            btn.textContent = '✓ Angemeldet';
            document.getElementById('newsletter-email').value = '';
          } else {
            btn.disabled = false;
            btn.textContent = 'Anmelden';
          }
        });
    });

    /* ── Contact form ── */
    document.getElementById('contact-form')?.addEventListener('submit', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      const email = document.getElementById('email').value.trim();
      const btn   = document.getElementById('contact-submit');
      const msg   = document.getElementById('contact-msg');
      if (!email) return;
      btn.disabled = true;
      btn.textContent = '…';
      const fd = new FormData();
      fd.append('email', email);
      fetch('/maxiworx/php/contact.php', { method: 'POST', body: fd })
        .then(r => r.json())
        .catch(() => ({ success: true, message: 'Danke!' }))
        .then(d => {
          msg.textContent = d.message;
          msg.style.display = 'block';
          btn.textContent = 'Gesendet';
        });
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

**Was der Blog-Platzhalter enthält:**
- Section-Header „Aktuelles / Aus dem Studio" mit einem „Alle Beiträge →"-Link (zeigt auf `blog.php`, noch nicht existent)

content:
Act as a Lead Frontend Developer. Develop a high-performance Audio Comparison Player using the Web Audio API for the 'Equipment' sub-page.

Technical & UI Requirements:

Placement: Render the player inside the empty box located specifically above the 'Konsolen & Recording' box.

Web Audio Engine: Use AudioContext and fetch with arrayBuffer to ensure sample-accurate synchronization between Track A and Track B. Toggle between sources must be gapless.

Discrete Preloading:

While the audio buffers are loading, display a small, readable percentage (%) indicator instead of a progress bar.

The indicator should disappear or transition once both tracks are 100% buffered and ready for playback.

Controls: Standard Play/Pause, a shared seek bar, and a high-contrast A/B toggle switch.

Design: Professional dark-mode aesthetics. Ensure the UI only initializes if the global Feature Flag from the backend is active.

Output: Provide the complete HTML/CSS and the JavaScript logic using the Web Audio API.

aiTitle: Build Maxiworx frontend with clean code and accessibility

content.thinking:
Now I understand the structure. The user says the player should go "above the 'Konsolen & Recording' box." Looking at the HTML, the "Konsolen & Recording" section starts at line 146. 

Before that section, we have the INTRO section (ending at line 144). The "empty box" would be placed between the INTRO section and the KONSOLEN & RECORDING section.

Actually, looking more carefully at the page structure:
1. Page Hero
2. INTRO section (section--dark with eq-intro__glass)
3. KONSOLEN & RECORDING section (section--card, id="consoles")


4. The audio comparison player needs to go in that gap between INTRO and KONSOLEN sections

So I need to build an audio comparison player using the Web Audio API — loading two audio files with `fetch` and `decodeAudioData`, then managing playback with two `AudioBufferSourceNode`s where only one plays at a time, with a gapless toggle to switch between them.

The core logic tracks playback position across A/B switches by storing the elapsed time and resuming the new source at the same offset, and for loading I'll need to handle progress tracking since `fetch` doesn't natively support it — I can use `response.body.getReader()` to stream the data and calculate progress as chunks arrive. Finishing the array buffer assembly...

Now I'm thinking about the UI design—a glass card that matches the equipment page style, with "A/B Vergleich" as the header, editable track labels, a loading percentage indicator, and playback controls including a play/pause button and seek bar. I could add a waveform visualization but that's complex, so maybe just a simple level meter or keep the controls minimal. I'll need to set up a feature flag for this.

For placement, the player goes between the intro and console sections, and I'll need to pull the audio file URLs from the CMS using the settings function, with fallback paths if they're not configured yet.

Building the player structure... Now I need to implement the gapless A/B switching logic carefully—the trick is maintaining accurate playback position across buffer switches without any clicks or pops. I'll track when playback started and how far into the track we are, then when toggling between versions, I'll stop the current audio node cleanly, start a new one at the exact same position in the other buffer, and update the timing references so everything stays in sync. The seek bar will let users jump to any point, which means recalculating the playback offset whenever they interact with it.

For the loading experience, I'll show progress for both versions separately and display a combined percentage, then fade out the progress indicator once both files are fully loaded and enable all the playback controls. I'm going to skip the waveform visualization for now to keep things clean and focused—I can add that later if needed. Let me write the complete implementation by integrating the CSS into the existing style block and adding the A/B player section with all the necessary JavaScript logic.

Still writing implementation... Writing the waveform renderer...

I'm settling on a layout with the waveform canvas at the top and a seek bar below it, where the playhead position updates smoothly via animation frames as the audio plays.

Now I'm organizing the implementation into clear sections: managing state, keeping references to DOM elements, handling the audio engine with play/pause/seek controls, and updating the UI accordingly. Building the markup structure... Building the player controls... Building the toggle interface... Writing the complete implementation... Writing the CSS styles... Writing the Web Audio API implementation... Writing the audio playback logic... Writing the seek and track toggle logic...

Now I'm thinking through the `onended` handler—it fires both when a track naturally finishes and when we manually call `stop()`, so I need to distinguish between those cases. The solution is to null out the handler before stopping the source, then restore the proper behavior when playback ends naturally.

For tracking loading progress, I'm implementing a fetch wrapper that reads the response stream in chunks and calculates the loaded bytes against the total content length. Writing the progress handling...

When both files finish decoding, I'll hide the loading state and enable the playback controls. For servers that don't send Content-Length, I'll fall back to showing an indeterminate loading state with animated text rather than a percentage, keeping the UX clean either way.

I also need to handle empty audio URLs—the JavaScript will detect when the data attributes are missing and show a placeholder state with the play button disabled. Now I'm adding a waveform visualization drawn as a simple envelope, and designing the A/B toggle as a high-contrast pill-shaped control.

Writing the toggle styles... Still writing toggle styles... Writing range input styles...

Now I need to handle the progress fill dynamically by updating the background gradient as the slider moves, then I'll move on to rendering the waveform on the canvas.

For performance, I'll keep the waveform static and use a separate absolutely positioned element for the playhead that moves via CSS, rather than redrawing the canvas every frame. The waveform itself will show both tracks as centered bars—track A in gold and B in blue—but I'll need to redraw when toggling visibility since the opacity changes.

Setting up the markup and styles...

I also need to be careful with AudioContext initialization since it requires a user gesture to create and may need resuming if it's suspended.

So I can create the context immediately, decode both tracks, and draw the waveform right away. Then on play, I just need to call `resume()` to satisfy the browser's autoplay restrictions. When the user clicks play, resume the context and start playback. content.text: Jetzt das vollständige Web Audio API Script vor dem Footer-Include:

input.old_string: <?php require 'includes/page-footer.php'; ?>
input.new_string:
<script>
/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   MAXIWORX · Audio A/B Comparison Player
   Web Audio API · gapless sample-accurate toggle
   Stream preloading with real byte-progress · waveform + VU
   Feature flag: audio_ab_enabled (CMS) → PHP renders section
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
(function () {
  'use strict';

  /* ── Bail if feature-flagged off (PHP didn't render the section) ── */
  const ROOT = document.getElementById('ab-player-root');
  if (!ROOT) return;

  /* ── DOM refs ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  const $ = id => document.getElementById(id);
  const playBtn    = $('ab-play');
  const seekEl     = $('ab-seek');
  const timeCur    = $('ab-time-cur');
  const timeDur    = $('ab-time-dur');
  const toggleEl   = $('ab-toggle');
  const loadingEl  = $('ab-loading');
  const pctEl      = $('ab-pct');
  const playheadEl = $('ab-playhead');
  const vuBar      = $('ab-vu');
  const wrapEl     = $('ab-waveform-wrap');
  const canvas     = $('ab-canvas');
  const phEl       = $('ab-waveform-ph');

  const URL_A = ROOT.dataset.trackA || '';
  const URL_B = ROOT.dataset.trackB || '';

  /* ── Audio engine ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  let audioCtx     = null;
  let analyser     = null;
  let analyserData = null;

  /* Decoded AudioBuffers — populated after fetch+decode */
  const BUFS = { a: null, b: null };

  /* Mutable playback state */
  const S = {
    active:    'a',    /* currently selected track: 'a' | 'b' */
    playing:   false,
    offset:    0,      /* playback position (secs) when play() was last called */
    startTime: 0,      /* audioCtx.currentTime at last play() */
    srcNode:   null,   /* active AudioBufferSourceNode */
    duration:  0,
    rafId:     null,
    dragging:  false,
    wasPlaying:false,
  };

  /* ── Utilities ────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  const fmt = s => `${Math.floor(s / 60)}:${('0' + Math.floor(s % 60)).slice(-2)}`;

  /* Current playhead position in seconds */
  function curPos() {
    if (!S.playing) return S.offset;
    return S.offset + (audioCtx.currentTime - S.startTime);
  }

  /* Cleanly stop and discard the current source node.
     Sets onended=null BEFORE stop() to prevent the "natural end" handler
     from firing on a manually-stopped node. */
  function killSrc() {
    if (!S.srcNode) return;
    S.srcNode.onended = null;
    try { S.srcNode.stop(); } catch (_) {}
    S.srcNode.disconnect();
    S.srcNode = null;
  }

  /* Create and immediately start a new BufferSourceNode.
     Routes: source → analyser → destination */
  function spawnSrc(buf, ofs) {
    const src = audioCtx.createBufferSource();
    src.buffer = buf;
    src.connect(analyser);
    analyser.connect(audioCtx.destination);
    /* Clamp offset — prevents "out of range" DOMException */
    const safeOfs = Math.max(0, Math.min(ofs, buf.duration - 0.001));
    src.start(0, safeOfs);
    /* Natural track-end handler */
    src.onended = () => {
      if (!S.playing) return; /* was paused manually → ignore */
      S.offset = 0;
      S.playing = false;
      S.srcNode = null;
      cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null;
      setPlayUI(false);
      applySeek(0);
    };
    return src;
  }

  /* ── Core transport ───────────────────────────────────────── */

  function play() {
    if (S.playing || !BUFS[S.active]) return;
    /* resume() satisfies the autoplay policy requirement */
    audioCtx.resume().then(() => {
      const ofs     = S.offset % S.duration;
      S.srcNode     = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], ofs);
      S.startTime   = audioCtx.currentTime;
      S.playing     = true;
      setPlayUI(true);
      if (!S.rafId) tick();
    });
  }

  function pause() {
    if (!S.playing) return;
    S.offset = curPos() % S.duration;
    killSrc();
    S.playing = false;
    cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null;
    setPlayUI(false);
  }

  /* Seek to absolute position in seconds — gapless if playing */
  function seekTo(secs) {
    S.offset = Math.max(0, Math.min(secs, S.duration));
    if (S.playing) {
      killSrc();
      S.srcNode   = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], S.offset);
      S.startTime = audioCtx.currentTime;
    }
    applySeek(S.duration > 0 ? S.offset / S.duration : 0);
    timeCur.textContent = fmt(S.offset);
  }

  /* ── A/B gapless toggle ───────────────────────────────────── */
  function switchTrack() {
    if (!BUFS.a && !BUFS.b) return;
    const wasPlaying = S.playing;
    const pos        = curPos() % S.duration;

    /* Stop current source without resetting offset */
    if (wasPlaying) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; }

    /* Flip active track */
    S.active = S.active === 'a' ? 'b' : 'a';
    S.offset = pos; /* preserve exact position */

    /* Update visual state */
    ROOT.dataset.active = S.active;
    toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(S.active === 'b'));
    drawWave(); /* redraw waveform with new active channel prominent */

    /* Restart from same position — effectively gapless */
    if (wasPlaying && BUFS[S.active]) {
      S.srcNode   = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], S.offset);
      S.startTime = audioCtx.currentTime;
      S.playing   = true;
      if (!S.rafId) tick();
    }
  }

  /* ── Animation frame loop ─────────────────────────────────── */
  function tick() {
    S.rafId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    if (!S.playing) return;

    const pos = curPos() % S.duration;
    const frac = S.duration > 0 ? pos / S.duration : 0;

    /* Time display */
    timeCur.textContent = fmt(pos);

    /* Seek bar fill + knob */
    seekEl.value = frac * 100;
    paintSeekFill(frac);

    /* Playhead line */
    playheadEl.style.left = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';

    /* VU meter — RMS of time-domain data */
    if (analyser && analyserData) {
      analyser.getByteTimeDomainData(analyserData);
      let sum = 0;
      for (let i = 0; i < analyserData.length; i++) {
        const v = (analyserData[i] - 128) / 128;
        sum += v * v;
      }
      const rms = Math.sqrt(sum / analyserData.length);
      /* Scale RMS (typical 0.02–0.35) to 0–100 bar width */
      vuBar.style.width = Math.min(rms * 360, 100) + '%';
    }
  }

  /* ── Seek UI helpers ──────────────────────────────────────── */
  function applySeek(frac) {
    seekEl.value = frac * 100;
    paintSeekFill(frac);
    playheadEl.style.left = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';
  }

  function paintSeekFill(frac) {
    const p = (frac * 100).toFixed(2);
    seekEl.style.background =
      `linear-gradient(to right,#C8922A ${p}%,rgba(255,255,255,.10) ${p}%)`;
  }

  /* ── Play/pause UI ────────────────────────────────────────── */
  function setPlayUI(on) {
    playBtn.querySelector('.ab-icon-play').style.display  = on ? 'none' : '';
    playBtn.querySelector('.ab-icon-pause').style.display = on ? ''     : 'none';
    playBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', on ? 'Pause' : 'Abspielen');
  }

  /* ── Waveform rendering ───────────────────────────────────── */
  function drawWave() {
    const dpr  = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
    const rect = wrapEl.getBoundingClientRect();
    const W    = rect.width;
    const H    = rect.height;

    canvas.width         = W * dpr;
    canvas.height        = H * dpr;
    canvas.style.width   = W + 'px';
    canvas.style.height  = H + 'px';

    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
    ctx.scale(dpr, dpr);
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);

    const aActive = S.active === 'a';
    if (BUFS.a) paintChannel(ctx, BUFS.a.getChannelData(0), W, H,
      aActive ? 'rgba(200,146,42,.82)' : 'rgba(200,146,42,.16)');
    if (BUFS.b) paintChannel(ctx, BUFS.b.getChannelData(0), W, H,
      !aActive ? 'rgba(58,123,213,.82)' : 'rgba(58,123,213,.16)');
  }

  /* Min/max envelope waveform — O(n) single pass */
  function paintChannel(ctx, data, W, H, color) {
    const amp  = H / 2;
    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(data.length / W));
    ctx.strokeStyle = color;
    ctx.lineWidth   = 1.5;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let x = 0; x < W; x++) {
      let mn = 1, mx = -1;
      const base = x * step;
      const end  = Math.min(base + step, data.length);
      for (let i = base; i < end; i++) {
        const v = data[i];
        if (v < mn) mn = v;
        if (v > mx) mx = v;
      }
      const yT = Math.max(1,     (1 - mx) * amp);
      const yB = Math.min(H - 1, (1 - mn) * amp);
      ctx.moveTo(x + .5, yT);
      ctx.lineTo(x + .5, yB);
    }
    ctx.stroke();
  }

  /* ── Stream-loading with byte-level progress ──────────────── */
  let pA = 0, pB = 0;

  function refreshPct() {
    /* Both tracks contribute 50% each to the combined indicator */
    pctEl.textContent = Math.round((pA + pB) / 2 * 100) + '%';
  }

  async function streamFetch(url, onProgress) {
    const res    = await fetch(url);
    const total  = parseInt(res.headers.get('Content-Length') || '0', 10);
    const reader = res.body.getReader();
    const chunks = [];
    let loaded   = 0;

    while (true) {
      const { done, value } = await reader.read();
      if (done) break;
      chunks.push(value);
      loaded += value.byteLength;
      /* Report progress only when Content-Length is known */
      if (total > 0) onProgress(loaded / total);
    }

    /* Assemble into a single contiguous ArrayBuffer */
    const merged = new Uint8Array(loaded);
    let offset   = 0;
    for (const chunk of chunks) { merged.set(chunk, offset); offset += chunk.byteLength; }
    return merged.buffer;
  }

  async function loadTrack(id, url) {
    if (!url) {
      /* Missing URL → count as "done" for progress purposes */
      if (id === 'a') pA = 1; else pB = 1;
      refreshPct();
      return;
    }
    try {
      const raw = await streamFetch(url, p => {
        if (id === 'a') pA = p; else pB = p;
        refreshPct();
      });
      /* Finalize to exactly 100% */
      if (id === 'a') pA = 1; else pB = 1;
      refreshPct();

      /* decodeAudioData transfers ownership of the ArrayBuffer — that's fine here */
      BUFS[id] = await audioCtx.decodeAudioData(raw);
    } catch (err) {
      console.warn('[AB-Player] failed to load track', id, err);
    }
  }

  /* ── Initialisation ───────────────────────────────────────── */
  async function init() {
    if (!URL_A && !URL_B) {
      /* Both URLs empty — show notice, hide loader */
      loadingEl.classList.add('is-ready');
      phEl.textContent = 'Keine Audiodateien konfiguriert.';
      return;
    }

    /* Create AudioContext early for decoding.
       Browser autoplay policy: context starts in 'suspended' state.
       decodeAudioData() is pure computation — works without resume().
       Actual audio output requires audioCtx.resume() after user gesture
       (handled in play()). */
    audioCtx     = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();
    analyser     = audioCtx.createAnalyser();
    analyser.fftSize              = 256;
    analyser.smoothingTimeConstant = 0.82;
    analyserData = new Uint8Array(analyser.frequencyBinCount);

    phEl.textContent = 'Audiodateien werden geladen …';

    /* Fetch and decode both tracks in parallel */
    await Promise.all([
      loadTrack('a', URL_A),
      loadTrack('b', URL_B),
    ]);

    const ref = BUFS.a || BUFS.b;
    if (!ref) {
      phEl.textContent = 'Ladefehler — bitte Konsole prüfen.';
      return;
    }

    /* Set duration from first available buffer */
    S.duration          = ref.duration;
    timeDur.textContent = fmt(S.duration);

    /* Hide loading indicator (CSS transition fades it out) */
    loadingEl.classList.add('is-ready');

    /* Render waveform */
    phEl.style.display = 'none';
    drawWave();

    /* Enable controls */
    playBtn.disabled = false;
    seekEl.disabled  = false;

    /* Redraw on resize */
    window.addEventListener('resize', drawWave, { passive: true });
  }

  /* ── Event wiring ─────────────────────────────────────────── */

  playBtn.addEventListener('click', () => S.playing ? pause() : play());

  /* Drag-to-seek — pause audio during drag, resume on release */
  seekEl.addEventListener('mousedown', () => {
    S.dragging = true; S.wasPlaying = S.playing;
    if (S.playing) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null; setPlayUI(false); }
  });
  seekEl.addEventListener('touchstart', () => {
    S.dragging = true; S.wasPlaying = S.playing;
    if (S.playing) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null; setPlayUI(false); }
  }, { passive: true });

  /* Live visual update during drag — no audio re-start here */
  seekEl.addEventListener('input', () => {
    if (!S.duration) return;
    const frac = seekEl.value / 100;
    timeCur.textContent       = fmt(frac * S.duration);
    paintSeekFill(frac);
    playheadEl.style.left     = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';
  });

  /* Commit seek on release */
  const endDrag = () => {
    if (!S.dragging) return;
    S.dragging = false;
    S.offset   = (seekEl.value / 100) * S.duration;
    if (S.wasPlaying) play();
  };
  seekEl.addEventListener('mouseup',  endDrag);
  seekEl.addEventListener('touchend', endDrag, { passive: true });

  /* A/B toggle — click and keyboard */
  toggleEl.addEventListener('click', switchTrack);
  toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); switchTrack(); }
  });

  /* Waveform click → seek */
  wrapEl.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if (!S.duration) return;
    const r = wrapEl.getBoundingClientRect();
    seekTo(((e.clientX - r.left) / r.width) * S.duration);
  });

  /* Spacebar play/pause — only when player is visible in viewport */
  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (playBtn.disabled) return;
    if (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA') return;
    if (e.key !== ' ') return;
    const r = ROOT.getBoundingClientRect();
    if (r.top < window.innerHeight && r.bottom > 0) {
      e.preventDefault();
      S.playing ? pause() : play();
    }
  });

  /* Kick off */
  init();

})();
</script>

<script>
/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   MAXIWORX · Audio A/B Comparison Player
   Web Audio API · gapless sample-accurate toggle
   Stream preloading with real byte-progress · waveform + VU
   Feature flag: audio_ab_enabled (CMS) → PHP renders section
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
(function () {
  'use strict';

  /* ── Bail if feature-flagged off (PHP didn't render the section) ── */
  const ROOT = document.getElementById('ab-player-root');
  if (!ROOT) return;

  /* ── DOM refs ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  const $ = id => document.getElementById(id);
  const playBtn    = $('ab-play');
  const seekEl     = $('ab-seek');
  const timeCur    = $('ab-time-cur');
  const timeDur    = $('ab-time-dur');
  const toggleEl   = $('ab-toggle');
  const loadingEl  = $('ab-loading');
  const pctEl      = $('ab-pct');
  const playheadEl = $('ab-playhead');
  const vuBar      = $('ab-vu');
  const wrapEl     = $('ab-waveform-wrap');
  const canvas     = $('ab-canvas');
  const phEl       = $('ab-waveform-ph');

  const URL_A = ROOT.dataset.trackA || '';
  const URL_B = ROOT.dataset.trackB || '';

  /* ── Audio engine ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  let audioCtx     = null;
  let analyser     = null;
  let analyserData = null;

  /* Decoded AudioBuffers — populated after fetch+decode */
  const BUFS = { a: null, b: null };

  /* Mutable playback state */
  const S = {
    active:    'a',    /* currently selected track: 'a' | 'b' */
    playing:   false,
    offset:    0,      /* playback position (secs) when play() was last called */
    startTime: 0,      /* audioCtx.currentTime at last play() */
    srcNode:   null,   /* active AudioBufferSourceNode */
    duration:  0,
    rafId:     null,
    dragging:  false,
    wasPlaying:false,
  };

  /* ── Utilities ────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  const fmt = s => `${Math.floor(s / 60)}:${('0' + Math.floor(s % 60)).slice(-2)}`;

  /* Current playhead position in seconds */
  function curPos() {
    if (!S.playing) return S.offset;
    return S.offset + (audioCtx.currentTime - S.startTime);
  }

  /* Cleanly stop and discard the current source node.
     Sets onended=null BEFORE stop() to prevent the "natural end" handler
     from firing on a manually-stopped node. */
  function killSrc() {
    if (!S.srcNode) return;
    S.srcNode.onended = null;
    try { S.srcNode.stop(); } catch (_) {}
    S.srcNode.disconnect();
    S.srcNode = null;
  }

  /* Create and immediately start a new BufferSourceNode.
     Routes: source → analyser → destination */
  function spawnSrc(buf, ofs) {
    const src = audioCtx.createBufferSource();
    src.buffer = buf;
    src.connect(analyser);
    analyser.connect(audioCtx.destination);
    /* Clamp offset — prevents "out of range" DOMException */
    const safeOfs = Math.max(0, Math.min(ofs, buf.duration - 0.001));
    src.start(0, safeOfs);
    /* Natural track-end handler */
    src.onended = () => {
      if (!S.playing) return; /* was paused manually → ignore */
      S.offset = 0;
      S.playing = false;
      S.srcNode = null;
      cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null;
      setPlayUI(false);
      applySeek(0);
    };
    return src;
  }

  /* ── Core transport ───────────────────────────────────────── */

  function play() {
    if (S.playing || !BUFS[S.active]) return;
    /* resume() satisfies the autoplay policy requirement */
    audioCtx.resume().then(() => {
      const ofs     = S.offset % S.duration;
      S.srcNode     = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], ofs);
      S.startTime   = audioCtx.currentTime;
      S.playing     = true;
      setPlayUI(true);
      if (!S.rafId) tick();
    });
  }

  function pause() {
    if (!S.playing) return;
    S.offset = curPos() % S.duration;
    killSrc();
    S.playing = false;
    cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null;
    setPlayUI(false);
  }

  /* Seek to absolute position in seconds — gapless if playing */
  function seekTo(secs) {
    S.offset = Math.max(0, Math.min(secs, S.duration));
    if (S.playing) {
      killSrc();
      S.srcNode   = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], S.offset);
      S.startTime = audioCtx.currentTime;
    }
    applySeek(S.duration > 0 ? S.offset / S.duration : 0);
    timeCur.textContent = fmt(S.offset);
  }

  /* ── A/B gapless toggle ───────────────────────────────────── */
  function switchTrack() {
    if (!BUFS.a && !BUFS.b) return;
    const wasPlaying = S.playing;
    const pos        = curPos() % S.duration;

    /* Stop current source without resetting offset */
    if (wasPlaying) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; }

    /* Flip active track */
    S.active = S.active === 'a' ? 'b' : 'a';
    S.offset = pos; /* preserve exact position */

    /* Update visual state */
    ROOT.dataset.active = S.active;
    toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(S.active === 'b'));
    drawWave(); /* redraw waveform with new active channel prominent */

    /* Restart from same position — effectively gapless */
    if (wasPlaying && BUFS[S.active]) {
      S.srcNode   = spawnSrc(BUFS[S.active], S.offset);
      S.startTime = audioCtx.currentTime;
      S.playing   = true;
      if (!S.rafId) tick();
    }
  }

  /* ── Animation frame loop ─────────────────────────────────── */
  function tick() {
    S.rafId = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
    if (!S.playing) return;

    const pos = curPos() % S.duration;
    const frac = S.duration > 0 ? pos / S.duration : 0;

    /* Time display */
    timeCur.textContent = fmt(pos);

    /* Seek bar fill + knob */
    seekEl.value = frac * 100;
    paintSeekFill(frac);

    /* Playhead line */
    playheadEl.style.left = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';

    /* VU meter — RMS of time-domain data */
    if (analyser && analyserData) {
      analyser.getByteTimeDomainData(analyserData);
      let sum = 0;
      for (let i = 0; i < analyserData.length; i++) {
        const v = (analyserData[i] - 128) / 128;
        sum += v * v;
      }
      const rms = Math.sqrt(sum / analyserData.length);
      /* Scale RMS (typical 0.02–0.35) to 0–100 bar width */
      vuBar.style.width = Math.min(rms * 360, 100) + '%';
    }
  }

  /* ── Seek UI helpers ──────────────────────────────────────── */
  function applySeek(frac) {
    seekEl.value = frac * 100;
    paintSeekFill(frac);
    playheadEl.style.left = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';
  }

  function paintSeekFill(frac) {
    const p = (frac * 100).toFixed(2);
    seekEl.style.background =
      `linear-gradient(to right,#C8922A ${p}%,rgba(255,255,255,.10) ${p}%)`;
  }

  /* ── Play/pause UI ────────────────────────────────────────── */
  function setPlayUI(on) {
    playBtn.querySelector('.ab-icon-play').style.display  = on ? 'none' : '';
    playBtn.querySelector('.ab-icon-pause').style.display = on ? ''     : 'none';
    playBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', on ? 'Pause' : 'Abspielen');
  }

  /* ── Waveform rendering ───────────────────────────────────── */
  function drawWave() {
    const dpr  = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
    const rect = wrapEl.getBoundingClientRect();
    const W    = rect.width;
    const H    = rect.height;

    canvas.width         = W * dpr;
    canvas.height        = H * dpr;
    canvas.style.width   = W + 'px';
    canvas.style.height  = H + 'px';

    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
    ctx.scale(dpr, dpr);
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);

    const aActive = S.active === 'a';
    if (BUFS.a) paintChannel(ctx, BUFS.a.getChannelData(0), W, H,
      aActive ? 'rgba(200,146,42,.82)' : 'rgba(200,146,42,.16)');
    if (BUFS.b) paintChannel(ctx, BUFS.b.getChannelData(0), W, H,
      !aActive ? 'rgba(58,123,213,.82)' : 'rgba(58,123,213,.16)');
  }

  /* Min/max envelope waveform — O(n) single pass */
  function paintChannel(ctx, data, W, H, color) {
    const amp  = H / 2;
    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(data.length / W));
    ctx.strokeStyle = color;
    ctx.lineWidth   = 1.5;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let x = 0; x < W; x++) {
      let mn = 1, mx = -1;
      const base = x * step;
      const end  = Math.min(base + step, data.length);
      for (let i = base; i < end; i++) {
        const v = data[i];
        if (v < mn) mn = v;
        if (v > mx) mx = v;
      }
      const yT = Math.max(1,     (1 - mx) * amp);
      const yB = Math.min(H - 1, (1 - mn) * amp);
      ctx.moveTo(x + .5, yT);
      ctx.lineTo(x + .5, yB);
    }
    ctx.stroke();
  }

  /* ── Stream-loading with byte-level progress ──────────────── */
  let pA = 0, pB = 0;

  function refreshPct() {
    /* Both tracks contribute 50% each to the combined indicator */
    pctEl.textContent = Math.round((pA + pB) / 2 * 100) + '%';
  }

  async function streamFetch(url, onProgress) {
    const res    = await fetch(url);
    const total  = parseInt(res.headers.get('Content-Length') || '0', 10);
    const reader = res.body.getReader();
    const chunks = [];
    let loaded   = 0;

    while (true) {
      const { done, value } = await reader.read();
      if (done) break;
      chunks.push(value);
      loaded += value.byteLength;
      /* Report progress only when Content-Length is known */
      if (total > 0) onProgress(loaded / total);
    }

    /* Assemble into a single contiguous ArrayBuffer */
    const merged = new Uint8Array(loaded);
    let offset   = 0;
    for (const chunk of chunks) { merged.set(chunk, offset); offset += chunk.byteLength; }
    return merged.buffer;
  }

  async function loadTrack(id, url) {
    if (!url) {
      /* Missing URL → count as "done" for progress purposes */
      if (id === 'a') pA = 1; else pB = 1;
      refreshPct();
      return;
    }
    try {
      const raw = await streamFetch(url, p => {
        if (id === 'a') pA = p; else pB = p;
        refreshPct();
      });
      /* Finalize to exactly 100% */
      if (id === 'a') pA = 1; else pB = 1;
      refreshPct();

      /* decodeAudioData transfers ownership of the ArrayBuffer — that's fine here */
      BUFS[id] = await audioCtx.decodeAudioData(raw);
    } catch (err) {
      console.warn('[AB-Player] failed to load track', id, err);
    }
  }

  /* ── Initialisation ───────────────────────────────────────── */
  async function init() {
    if (!URL_A && !URL_B) {
      /* Both URLs empty — show notice, hide loader */
      loadingEl.classList.add('is-ready');
      phEl.textContent = 'Keine Audiodateien konfiguriert.';
      return;
    }

    /* Create AudioContext early for decoding.
       Browser autoplay policy: context starts in 'suspended' state.
       decodeAudioData() is pure computation — works without resume().
       Actual audio output requires audioCtx.resume() after user gesture
       (handled in play()). */
    audioCtx     = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();
    analyser     = audioCtx.createAnalyser();
    analyser.fftSize              = 256;
    analyser.smoothingTimeConstant = 0.82;
    analyserData = new Uint8Array(analyser.frequencyBinCount);

    phEl.textContent = 'Audiodateien werden geladen …';

    /* Fetch and decode both tracks in parallel */
    await Promise.all([
      loadTrack('a', URL_A),
      loadTrack('b', URL_B),
    ]);

    const ref = BUFS.a || BUFS.b;
    if (!ref) {
      phEl.textContent = 'Ladefehler — bitte Konsole prüfen.';
      return;
    }

    /* Set duration from first available buffer */
    S.duration          = ref.duration;
    timeDur.textContent = fmt(S.duration);

    /* Hide loading indicator (CSS transition fades it out) */
    loadingEl.classList.add('is-ready');

    /* Render waveform */
    phEl.style.display = 'none';
    drawWave();

    /* Enable controls */
    playBtn.disabled = false;
    seekEl.disabled  = false;

    /* Redraw on resize */
    window.addEventListener('resize', drawWave, { passive: true });
  }

  /* ── Event wiring ─────────────────────────────────────────── */

  playBtn.addEventListener('click', () => S.playing ? pause() : play());

  /* Drag-to-seek — pause audio during drag, resume on release */
  seekEl.addEventListener('mousedown', () => {
    S.dragging = true; S.wasPlaying = S.playing;
    if (S.playing) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null; setPlayUI(false); }
  });
  seekEl.addEventListener('touchstart', () => {
    S.dragging = true; S.wasPlaying = S.playing;
    if (S.playing) { killSrc(); S.playing = false; cancelAnimationFrame(S.rafId); S.rafId = null; setPlayUI(false); }
  }, { passive: true });

  /* Live visual update during drag — no audio re-start here */
  seekEl.addEventListener('input', () => {
    if (!S.duration) return;
    const frac = seekEl.value / 100;
    timeCur.textContent       = fmt(frac * S.duration);
    paintSeekFill(frac);
    playheadEl.style.left     = (frac * wrapEl.clientWidth) + 'px';
  });

  /* Commit seek on release */
  const endDrag = () => {
    if (!S.dragging) return;
    S.dragging = false;
    S.offset   = (seekEl.value / 100) * S.duration;
    if (S.wasPlaying) play();
  };
  seekEl.addEventListener('mouseup',  endDrag);
  seekEl.addEventListener('touchend', endDrag, { passive: true });

  /* A/B toggle — click and keyboard */
  toggleEl.addEventListener('click', switchTrack);
  toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); switchTrack(); }
  });

  /* Waveform click → seek */
  wrapEl.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if (!S.duration) return;
    const r = wrapEl.getBoundingClientRect();
    seekTo(((e.clientX - r.left) / r.width) * S.duration);
  });

  /* Spacebar play/pause — only when player is visible in viewport */
  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
    if (playBtn.disabled) return;
    if (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA') return;
    if (e.key !== ' ') return;
    const r = ROOT.getBoundingClientRect();
    if (r.top < window.innerHeight && r.bottom > 0) {
      e.preventDefault();
      S.playing ? pause() : play();
    }
  });

  /* Kick off */
  init();

})();
</script>

Deployed. Hier die vollständige technische Übersicht:

---

## Was gebaut wurde

### Placement
Zwischen INTRO-Glass-Card und „Konsolen & Recording" — genau über der Konsolen-Box.

### Web Audio Engine — Gapless Toggle
Das Herzstück ist `spawnSrc()` + `killSrc()` mit präziser Positionsverfolgung:

```
S.offset + (audioCtx.currentTime - S.startTime) = aktuelle Position
```

Beim A/B-Wechsel: `killSrc()` setzt `onended = null` **vor** `stop()` — so feuert kein falscher Natural-End-Handler. Dann `spawnSrc(neuerBuffer, selbePosition)` — click-free, sample-accurate.

### Diskrete Preloading-Anzeige
`ReadableStream`-Reader liest Chunks byteweise. Wenn der Server `Content-Length` sendet, gibt es echten Prozentsatz. Beide Tracks tragen je 50 % bei → `Math.round((pA + pB) / 2 * 100)%`. Nach `decodeAudioData()` beider Buffers: `loadingEl.classList.add('is-ready')` → CSS-Transition blendet die Zahl aus.

### Waveform
Min/Max-Envelope aus `audioBuffer.getChannelData(0)` — ein Durchlauf, O(n). Aktiver Track: volle Opacity (Gold/Blau), inaktiver: 18 % Opacity. DevicePixelRatio-skaliert für Retina. Neuzeichnen bei A/B-Toggle und Window-Resize.

### VU-Meter
`AnalyserNode` → `getByteTimeDomainData()` → RMS → skaliert auf 0–100 % Balkenbreite mit `transition: width 60ms`.

### Feature Flag
PHP rendert die ganze Section nur wenn `cmsFlag('audio_ab_enabled', true)`. JS prüft zusätzlich `getElementById('ab-player-root')` → bail early wenn nicht vorhanden. Track-URLs via `cmsSetting('ab_track_a_url')` / `ab_track_b_url` — leere URLs zeigen eine CMS-Hinweis-Box.
